mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and rising consumer prices compress household purchasing power, forcing consumers to cut discretionary spending and reducing corporate earnings expectations, yielding negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually while RSAFS declines, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term momentum in equity markets during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 5-day returns when the economy is in a mature expansion phase, yielding an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next three months.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for quarterly equity returns across multiple business cycles, with a cumulative abnormal return indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 500,000 units signals a severe contraction in residential construction and related supply chains, dragging down equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 70% of months where housing starts breach the 500k threshold, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when payroll growth is driven solely by temporary government hiring, resulting in a negative Sharpe ratio for the strategy.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and prompts expectations of tighter financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during periods of constrained monetary policy.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes consistently coincide with positive quarterly equity returns when central banks maintain accommodative stances, producing a positive Rank IC above 0.05.
